tldr: Headlines across 21 press systems frame this story as a cycle of mutual military strikes—US airstrikes for the tenth consecutive night, Iranian counter-strikes across Gulf allies—layered with explicit punitive rhetoric (Trump 'vows revenge,' Iran 'will pay'). The Strait of Hormuz tanker attacks surface as the second-most visible headline hook. A narrow set of outlets (Haaretz, The New Arab, The Guardian) break from the military-action frame to foreground Pentagon casualty concealment; the diplomatic collapse and Pakistan mediation visible in body text are almost entirely absent from the headline layer.

frames.sub_frame: sustained US-Iran strikes across Gulf region

evidence.quote: US attacks Iran for 10th consecutive night
IRGC pounds US radar, air defense systems
US Completes Eighth Night of Strikes Against Iranian Military

frames.sub_frame: US vows revenge; Iran 'will pay' for troop deaths

evidence.quote: 'Iran Would Pay Many Times Over': Trump's Big Warning
US launches fresh strikes as Trump vows revenge
Tanker attacked in Strait of Hormuz
Tehran strikes tanker in the Strait of Hormuz
Tanker crew abandons vessel after reported projectile strike in Strait
Pentagon concealed dozens of U.S. troop injuries
Pentagon conceals dozens of US military casualties
Almost 100 troops injured in recent weeks, Pentagon admits
Is an Attack on Civilians' Basic Needs

single_outlet_findings.finding: Headline applies 'retaliatory' to Iranian strikes rather than US ones ('killed in Iranian retaliatory strikes'), inverting the aggressor/respondent framing used by virtually every other outlet.

Only US headline to lead with civilian harm: 'War's Escalation in Iran Is an Attack on Civilians' Basic Needs' — absent from every other outlet's title, including other US progressive and left-liberal feeds.
Only headline to surface the potential ground-war dimension: 'US strikes cut roads and bridges in southern Iran as ground assault talk grows' — absent from all other 62 titles.

Only headline to report Trump threatening Iranian power plants: 'Trump resumes Iran's naval blockade, threatens strikes on power plants' — a coercive ultimatum angle omitted from Western, Gulf, and Indian headlines.

One of the very few titles to place Houthi escalation in the headline: 'Iran targets US military assets as Houthis threaten to expand Mideast war' — giving the Houthi Saudi-port threat headline weight absent in most English-language Western press.

bottom_line: The headline layer presents this as a cycle of mutual military strikes—US retaliating for fallen troops, Iran counter-striking Gulf allies—with the Strait of Hormuz tanker attacks as the principal economic hook; the ceasefire collapse, Pakistan mediation, and civilian-harm dimensions prominent in body text are nearly invisible at the headline layer across all 21 press systems.
